steps.title: Recognize the vertex form structure
steps.content:
The maximum rate of change occurs at population 7,000 with value 0.200. For a quadratic model, the vertex form $r(x) = a(x - h)^2 + k$ directly encodes the vertex $(h, k)$. This is much faster than standard form, which requires solving a system of equations.

With vertex $(7000, 0.200)$:
$$r(x) = a(x - 7000)^2 + 0.200$$

steps.title: Find the leading coefficient using the point (0, 0)
steps.content:
Substitute the point where population = 0 and rate of change = 0:
$$0 = a(0 - 7000)^2 + 0.200$$
$$0 = a(7000)^2 + 0.200$$
$$a = -\frac{0.200}{(7000)^2}$$

The negative coefficient confirms the parabola opens downward, with its maximum at the vertex.

steps.title: Evaluate at population 700 using the distance ratio
